title: SDL state machine page
description: Machine-readable transcription of one SDL diagram from the AX.25 spec. Designed to be lossless w.r.t. the figure (minus physical layout) — the original diamond structure and original action ordering are preserved, so a figure can be redrawn from the YAML alone.

machine.description: Which spec state machine this page belongs to.

state.description: PascalCase name of the state this page describes.

coverage.description: 'partial' means this page covers only some of the state's transitions (e.g. one panel of a multi-panel SDL). 'complete' asserts every transition for this state appears here.

variables.description: Documentation-only list of variables this state reads/writes.

save.description: Events to be saved (deferred for processing in the next state) when received in this state. Corresponds to the SDL 'save' parallelogram shape (figc1.1).

decisions.description: Catalogue of decision diamonds appearing on this page. Each diamond has a stable id (referenced from transitions[].path[].decision), the question text as drawn in the figure, and a canonical boolean predicate evaluated at runtime.

question.description: Exact wording from the figure (e.g. 'Own Receiver Busy?').

predicate.description: Canonical boolean expression evaluated by the runtime GuardEvaluator (e.g. 'own_receiver_busy').

path.description: Ordered interleaving of decision-branch steps and action steps describing one column of the SDL figure. The codegen compiles this into a flat (guard, actions[]) pair for the runtime; the original ordering is preserved so the figure can be redrawn.

decision.description: References an id in the page-level decisions[] list.

branch.description: Which outgoing edge of the diamond this path follows. Only binary diamonds are supported today; if a 3-way decision appears in the spec we'll extend this enum.

action.description: Verb as drawn in the figure (e.g. 'RNR response', 'start_T1', 'DL_DISCONNECT_confirm').

kind.description: Which figc1.1 shape class produced this action. signal_upper=left-pointer parallelogram (to upper layer); signal_lower=right-pointer parallelogram (to lower layer); processing=plain rectangle (internal task); subroutine=rectangle with sidebars; internal_out=internal signal generation.
